final_response: Put it in `resources/prompt-cache-attack-paper.md` as a TLDR stub, added a followup to pull the actual PDF before next week, and logged it in today's daybook.

actions.summary: Created stub file holding the TLDR verbatim, flagged PDF-not-yet-pulled status, listed open items (fetch PDF, capture title/authors/link, verify 73% figure), noted promotion path to a subfolder if a thread builds.
Created today's daybook with reactive-day intent and a log entry noting the routing.
Added active entry: pull prompt-cache attack PDF by 2026-05-23, before the topic comes up next week.

notes: Routing rationale: TLDR is durable reference material the user wants to revisit, so `resources/` fits better than `tmp/` (would be lost) or a new `projects/` folder (single artifact, no work scope yet).
Did not create a project folder — one TLDR doesn't justify project scale. Stub file notes the promotion path to `resources/prompt-cache-attacks/` if more papers accumulate.
Did not write to `decisions.md` or `observations.md` — no commitment constraining future action and no descriptive pattern worth logging; this is routine intake.

Followup is self-directed (no external party), but still belongs in `followups.md` per its definition (an expectation that needs to come back); the by-date is anchored to the user's 'next week' framing.
